page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8163 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage options and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the instance delivers average multi-core performance but exhibits weaker single-core capabilities. In stress-ng testing, multi-core CPU performance scored in the middle 50% at 11773.91, while single-core performance fell into the bottom 25% at 1109.07. Similarly, Geekbench results show a weak single-core score of 958.0 and an average multi-core score of 5885.0. Memory performance is mixed, with uncached read and write bandwidths performing in the average tier at 71.56 GB/sec and 49.43 GB/sec, respectively, though the Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%.

Given its balanced resource allocation and average multi-core throughput, this instance is suitable for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, database operations, and moderate multi-threaded processing. It achieved average ratings in static web serving throughput at 7.93 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 11.76 million operations per second. However, single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and text processing are limited by the weaker single-core performance. The instance offers a stable, dedicated hardware profile for enterprise applications that require predictable CPU allocation without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated tiers.

Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average multi-core performance alongside weaker single-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core scoring in the middle tier and single-core in the bottom 25%. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded processing, where dedicated CPU allocation is required without specialized memory or storage demands.
